content: agent2: implementing process_priority param for click.edit() in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit function). Will touch these two files.

ack - noted you're touching Editor.edit_file->edit_files and edit() signature. My change: adding process_priority param to Editor.__init__ (new attribute, stored in self.process_priority) and applying it inside edit_file() around the subprocess.Popen call (computing creationflags for Windows before Popen, and os.setpriority after Popen on Unix) - I don't rename edit_file. In termui.py I'm just adding one new keyword-only-ish param 'process_priority: str | None = None' to edit()'s signature (appended at the end) and passing it to Editor(...). Should be additive and shouldn't conflict with your multi-file changes as long as edit_file's core Popen call structure stays recognizable. I'll ping you again before I finalize/submit patch.txt so we can diff-check overlap.

thanks - fyi in my copy of the repo, Editor.edit_file is still named edit_file (unchanged) and CHANGES.rst untouched, so looks like we're operating on independent repo copies rather than a shared filesystem - no literal file clobbering on my end. My diff only touches src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor.__init__ + edit_file, additive) and src/click/termui.py (edit() signature, additive kwarg at the end). Finalizing my patch now.
